manufacturer_evidence: Carny is DMS's high-performance all-rounder for average to better waves. Its slightly wider nose and clean outline into the tail are designed to carry smooth lines and real speed, while keeping paddling easy and turns punchy. The model blends modern design with performance intent, using normal rocker, normal rails and a single-to-double concave. It is meant to suit everyday Japanese-style average waves while still feeling sharp and performance oriented.
rocker_notes: The model blends modern design with performance intent, using normal rocker, normal rails and a single-to-double concave.

manufacturer_evidence: Gherkin sits in the DMS pocket-rocket lane and is built to make small-wave surfing lively. Rail volume is distributed from nose to tail to create quick take-offs and easy riding speed, while the distinctive crown-style tail helps the board pivot cleanly through short sharp turns. DMS highlights its strong match with a quad setup. The design uses a low rocker lifting through the tail, soft rails, and a single-to-double concave flowing into Vee, and it is positioned for 1-6ft waves.
rocker_notes: Rail volume is distributed from nose to tail to create quick take-offs and easy riding speed, while the distinctive crown-style tail helps the board pivot cleanly through short sharp turns. The design uses a low rocker lifting through the tail, soft rails, and a single-to-double concave flowing into Vee, and it is positioned for 1-6ft waves.

manufacturer_evidence: Seahawk is a twin-fin shape that chases both classic style and real performance surfing. It is designed for fast take-offs, smooth flowing lines, speed and easy rotation, giving the rider a connected feeling with the wave. DMS pairs the design with low rocker, normal rails, and single-to-double concave with rounded Vee. It is a versatile twin for 1-6ft surf with a strong speed-and-flow brief.
rocker_notes: DMS pairs the design with low rocker, normal rails, and single-to-double concave with rounded Vee.

manufacturer_evidence: Stilnox is one of DMS's fun-performance twin concepts, positioned on the more relaxed side of the range while still keeping drive and speed. DMS Japan describes it as a classic mini-Simmons influenced design that gains extra flex and smooth drive when built in Carbon Wrap. Independent write-ups describe it as a playful pointy twin with a little more rocker and more refined rails than ultra-flat mini-Simmons concepts, aimed at fast, fun walls and small-to-head-high conditions.
rocker_notes: Independent write-ups describe it as a playful pointy twin with a little more rocker and more refined rails than ultra-flat mini-Simmons concepts, aimed at fast, fun walls and small-to-head-high conditions.

manufacturer_evidence: Supercell is a true high-performance DMS shortboard for average to better waves. Compared with the Actor it softens the nose line, deepens the concave slightly and keeps the overall lift in a high-performance rocker so it can surf more vertically and aggressively in Japanese average waves. It comes alive when the surf has quality, hollow sections or more push. DMS pairs it with stronger rocker, soft-medium rails, and single-to-double concave flowing to Vee, and rates it for roughly 2-6ft conditions.
rocker_notes: Compared with the Actor it softens the nose line, deepens the concave slightly and keeps the overall lift in a high-performance rocker so it can surf more vertically and aggressively in Japanese average waves. DMS pairs it with stronger rocker, soft-medium rails, and single-to-double concave flowing to Vee, and rates it for roughly 2-6ft conditions.

manufacturer_evidence: Thunderbird is an all-round performance board that links a fuller, clean outline to a double-bump wing square tail. That tail layout helps it catch weaker Japanese small waves, drive through flatter sections and still hold a turning line when the surf gets bigger. DMS positions it as a versatile performer from onshore beach breaks through to head-overhead surf. It runs low rocker lifting through the tail, medium rails, and single-to-double concave into Vee.
rocker_notes: It runs low rocker lifting through the tail, medium rails, and single-to-double concave into Vee.

manufacturer_evidence: X-Wing takes Supercell performance DNA and adds a single-flyer channel concept to create a more aggressive but still controllable shortboard. It is aimed at surfers who still want performance but may not want an ultra-demanding high-performance board. DMS suggests riding it shorter than a regular board to unlock its design. The board uses normal rocker, normal rails, single and double concave with tail channels, and is positioned for 1-6ft waves.
rocker_notes: The board uses normal rocker, normal rails, single and double concave with tail channels, and is positioned for 1-6ft waves.

manufacturer_evidence: Zephyr blends 1970s single-fin heritage with a more modern shape to create a fast, gliding board that still draws smooth turns. DMS frames it as a stylish design that offers a stable take-off, down-the-line trimming speed and controlled glide. It uses low rocker, boxier rails and a flat-to-Vee bottom, making it an easy board to surf cleanly while still feeling classic and elegant.
rocker_notes: It uses low rocker, boxier rails and a flat-to-Vee bottom, making it an easy board to surf cleanly while still feeling classic and elegant.
